title: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training
exclusions: Does NOT cover general awareness training (covered by 22.46.1); focuses on privileged-role-specific training.
evidence: Saved search 'priv_role_training' (weekly) emits PAM-managed accounts whose owner lacks a current specialist certificate.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Security reminders and periodic training for workforce; extends to privileged roles.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA §164.308(a)(5)(ii)(A) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.46.3: Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.46.3' running on LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 12.6.3 annual security awareness training must cover role-specific risks.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An admin has no specialist training record in the last 365 days; the UC fires the next week.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All admins have a current specialist training record; no alert.

dataSources: LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping.
spl:
| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=*
| fields user account_type
| where account_type="privileged"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=priv_security_training earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user account_type last_completion
description: Provides a named list of privileged-role owners whose specialist training is overdue — a recurring theme in internal audits of ITGC.
value: Makes the 'role-based training' requirement evidence-backed rather than policy-on-paper.
implementation: (1) Tag PAM vault entries with the owner; (2) map course completions to users in the LMS; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-enrol overdue owners. Indexes required: `index=lms`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=completion`.
visualization: Table of overdue admins, bar chart of departments, single-value 'admins with current specialist training'. Key fields: `account_type`, `last_completion`, `user`, `user]`.
knownFalsePositives: Training completion gaps during onboarding waves, role transitions, or quarterly reminder cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag PAM vault entries with the owner; (2) map course completions to users in the LMS; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-enrol overdue owners.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=*
| fields user account_type
| where account_type="privileged"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=priv_security_training earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user account_type last_completion
```

Understanding this SPL

**Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training** — Makes the 'role-based training' requirement evidence-backed rather than policy-on-paper.

Documented **Data sources**: LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
• Filters the current rows with `where account_type="privileged"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: account_type, last_completion, user, user].

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of overdue admins, bar chart of departments, single-value 'admins with current specialist training'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see who still needs training so awareness stays current across teams and roles.
